ksa_text: Knowledge of training data integrity risks and mitigation, including data poisoning attack vectors, supply chain contamination, label manipulation, detection methodologies, and prevention controls for AI model training pipelines.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Security & Platform Strategy KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.428) to justify inclusion for the Data Platform Team Lead. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: This Knowledge KSA was excluded as an edge case, yet it has a composite score of 0.428 (moderate signal). Knowledge-dimension KSAs represent conceptual awareness rather than execution; exclusion may be too aggressive.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Overturn: moderate-signal Knowledge KSA in an edge domain still contributes to the role's awareness requirements. Revised to include.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.428). External validation of this knowledge KSA for the Data Platform Team Lead role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST CSF, CMU SEI enterprise security frameworks, and CDAIO job market data consistently include security and platform strategy oversight for senior data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Security & Platform Strategy for the Data Platform Team Lead is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Security & Platform Strategy knowledge KSA is relevant to the Data Platform Team Lead based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
